questions.stem: The open loop transfer function of a system with unity feedback is given by G(s) = K/(s(s+2)). Determine the gain K such that closed loop system has a damping ratio of 0.316. For this value of K, find rise time (0% to 100%), percentage overshoot, peak value of output, peak time and settling time (2% tolerance band), when the system is subjected to a step input of 12 units.
common_errors: Using 0% to 100% rise time formula but applying the 10%-90% formula instead
Forgetting to scale peak output by the step magnitude (12 units, not unit step)
Incorrect ωn from characteristic equation — missing the relationship ωn² = K
questions.solution_method: From CLTF characteristic equation s²+2s+K=0, equate 2ζωn=2 and ωn²=K; with ζ=0.316 get ωn and K. Then apply standard 2nd-order formulas: Mp=exp(-πζ/√(1-ζ²)), tp=π/ωd, ts=4/(ζωn), tr=(π-β)/ωd where β=cos⁻¹ζ. Scale peak output as (1+Mp)×12.

questions.stem: A unity-feedback closed loop control system is to be designed to meet the following specifications: 30% > Mp(%) > 15%, ts (2% tolerance band) < 0.75 sec. Mark the area in the s-plane in which the roots of the system's characteristic equation must lie. Assume the closed loop system is of second order.
common_errors: Confusing Mp bounds with ζ direction — higher Mp means lower ζ
Not drawing both the radial lines (ζ bounds) and vertical line (σ bound) together
Forgetting that the valid region must satisfy ALL constraints simultaneously (intersection, not union)
questions.solution_method: Convert Mp bounds to ζ bounds using ζ = -ln(Mp/100)/√(π²+ln²(Mp/100)): Mp=15% → ζ≈0.517, Mp=30% → ζ≈0.358. Convert ts bound to σ bound: σ = ζωn > 4/ts = 4/0.75 ≈ 5.33. Plot two radial lines at angles cos⁻¹(0.358) and cos⁻¹(0.517) from negative real axis, and a vertical line at σ=5.33; the feasible region is the intersection to the left of σ=5.33 between the two radial lines.
key_formulas: Angle of radial lines: θ = cos⁻¹(ζ)

questions.stem: For the system shown in Fig. Q3 (forward path: K/(Js+B) cascaded with 1/s, inner velocity feedback Kh), determine the values of K and Kh so that the maximum overshoot in the unit step response is 0.2 and the peak time is 1 sec. With these values of K and Kh, obtain the rise time and settling time. Assume J=1 kg-m², B=1 N-m/rad/sec.
common_errors: Not correctly reducing the inner velocity-feedback loop before computing the overall CLTF
Using Mp=0.2% instead of Mp=0.2 (20%) in the ζ formula
Forgetting B in the denominator when substituting J and B values
questions.solution_method: Reduce inner loop: inner CLTF = (K/(Js+B)) / (1 + Kh·K/(Js+B)) = K/(Js + B + KKh). Overall CLTF = [K/(Js+B+KKh)] / (s + K/(Js+B+KKh)) = K / (Js²+(B+KKh)s+K). With J=1, B=1: CLTF = K/(s²+(1+KKh)s+K). Equate ωn²=K and 2ζωn=(1+KKh). From Mp=0.2: ζ=0.456; from tp=1: ωd=π, ωn=π/√(1-ζ²)≈3.53. Then K=ωn²≈12.46, KKh=(2ζωn-1), solve Kh. Compute remaining specs from standard formulas.

notes: Single-page tutorial dated 24 February 2026. All 3 problems are second-order time-domain design/analysis. Q3 involves a tachometer/velocity-feedback inner loop requiring block-diagram reduction before applying standard 2nd-order formulas.
